title: Magnitude 7.2 earthquake shakes Aomori Prefecture in Japan, leaving several injured
summary: A strong earthquake with epicenter off the coast of Iwate caused injuries and material damage, but did not generate a tsunami.

title: Two earthquakes of magnitude greater than 7 shake Venezuela with at least 32 dead
summary: A double earthquake devastated densely populated residential areas, causing dozens of casualties and widespread damage.

title: Nikkei 225 index reaches new record high close at 72,366 points
summary: The Japanese stock market continued its rally driven by tech gains and a weak yen.

title: Bank of Japan considers raising interest rate to 2% with gradual increases
summary: Board member Tamura indicated that the central bank could raise the rate by 0.25 percentage points every few months.

title: Japan will face Brazil or Morocco in the World Cup round of 16 depending on its group position
summary: The Japanese team will define its rival in the next phase depending on whether it finishes first or second in Group F.

title: Chubu Electric shareholders' meeting approves re-election of chairman and CEO
summary: The annual general meeting rejected criticism over the data manipulation scandal and ratified top executives.

title: Shohei Ohtani announces birth of his second child, a boy
summary: The Dodgers player expressed his joy and left open the decision about his children's future in baseball.

title: Masayoshi Son, SoftBank founder, says he will remain at the helm of the company for another 15 years
summary: Son reaffirmed his commitment to turn SoftBank into a global leader in artificial intelligence.

title: Sentenced to 30 years in prison for the murderer of a university student in Hokkaido
summary: The Sapporo court handed down a sentence against the person responsible for the violent death of a student in an incident that shocked the community.

title: International Olympic Committee will award $10,000 to all athletes participating in the Games
summary: The direct aid program aims to support athletes worldwide so they can focus on their preparation.

title: Heavy rains and Typhoon No. 7 threaten western and eastern Japan
summary: Meteorological authorities warn of intense rainfall, landslides, and river flooding in wide areas of the country.

title: Brazil and Morocco advance to round of 16 as first and second in World Cup Group C
summary: The Brazilian team won its group after a 3-0 victory, while Morocco came from behind to qualify in second place.

title: Japanese companies adjust schedules so employees can watch Japan's World Cup match
summary: The initiative, supported by former footballer Keisuke Honda, aims to facilitate work concentration during the tournament.

title: Crude oil price falls to pre-conflict levels between the US and Iran
summary: Oil prices collapse amid easing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and expectations of increased supply.

title: Iran warns about maritime routes and US prepares new round of nuclear talks
summary: The Revolutionary Guards restrict navigation in the Persian Gulf while diplomats resume dialogue.

title: China detains two Japanese citizens on suspicion of smuggling
summary: Chinese authorities arrested two Japanese individuals for allegedly trying to take prohibited items out of the country.

title: Two years since the attack on Japanese mother and son in Suzhou, China
summary: Chinese authorities maintain surveillance in the area of the incident, which shocked the Japanese community.

title: Honda and Nissan agree to standardize key components for next-generation vehicles
summary: Despite the failure of the merger, both companies will continue collaborating to reduce costs on central parts.

title: Japan's nuclear regulatory commission reviews inspection process for atomic plants
summary: A two-phase evaluation will be established, analyzing seismic risk first before other safety aspects.

title: Japanese government allocates 115 billion yen to promote anime and manga abroad
summary: The plan includes support for translation and international events to promote Japanese pop culture.
